title: How AI changed the way I pick frameworks, and the two places React survived
excerpt: That 130-file PR that shipped KeyEcho 1.0 contained a decision I never wrote about: the desktop app...
content_html:
That 130-file PR that shipped KeyEcho 1.0 contained a decision I never wrote about: the desktop app moved from Tauri 1 + Vue to Tauri 2 + SolidJS.

The same summer, upweb.dev moved from Nuxt 4 to SolidStart, and keyecho.app (SSR, five languages, Stripe checkout) was built on SolidStart from scratch. Three separate decisions, same answer every time.

Scope first. This post is about defaults for my personal projects. Near the end I'll cover two places where I still use React, one of which runs very well.

The criteria changed

Most of the code in my repos is AI-generated now. My job has shifted from writing code to reviewing it.

The old framework checklist put most of its weight on learning curve and developer experience. Both are worth zero now: the model writes five frameworks fluently and knows the rules of hooks better than most humans do. AI crushed the price of writing code. Two costs didn't move: the runtime bytes every user downloads, and the human hours spent reviewing. Pick your stack by the new prices.

Runtime cost lives in the architecture. No prompt removes it, so it decides the framework. Review bandwidth is finite, and AI throughput will grind down any consistency that is maintained by verbal agreement, so it decides the styling layer. I have seen more than one React codebase a few years into its life: two animation libraries, two carousel components, three styling systems, each introduced for a perfectly good reason on some ticket, and the sum is a mess nobody can clean up. AI did not invent that drift. It made it an order of magnitude faster.

React is no longer my default: size and performance

Let me discard the weak arguments first. Dependency arrays, stale closures, re-render storms: I am not going to relitigate any of it. The model knows the rules of hooks cold, eslint-plugin-react-hooks catches most slips, and React Compiler now handles memoization. The ergonomics debate no longer produces a winner.

What still produces a winner is the client. react-dom is roughly 40 kB compressed, a virtual DOM plus a reconciler, downloaded, parsed, and executed on every cold visit by every user. React Compiler can remove wasted re-renders. It cannot remove the runtime itself. Developers never feel this cost. Users on phones pay it constantly.

My projects are one person plus a fleet of agents. Hiring pools do not help me, and content sites barely touch the component-library ecosystem. Once the weights shift like that, React has no slot left in my personal stack.

The same app, written ten times

Loren Stewart ran an experiment last year: the same kanban app implemented in ten frameworks, identical features (drag and drop, comments, tags, server validation), then compared compressed JS on the board page.

Framework Compressed JS
Next.js 16 (React 19) 176.1 kB
TanStack Start (React 19) 118.2 kB
Analog (Angular 20) 103.9 kB
Nuxt 4 (Vue 3) 74.7 kB
TanStack Start + Solid 60.4 kB
Qwik City 58.4 kB
SvelteKit (Svelte 5) 54.1 kB
SolidStart (SolidJS 1.9) 41.5 kB
Astro + HTMX 34.3 kB
Marko 28.8 kB

Two comparisons stand out. Same meta-framework, TanStack Start, with React swapped for Solid: 118.2 kB becomes 60.4 kB. Next.js against SolidStart: a 4.2x gap. His conclusion was that React's architectural cost is something no meta-framework can optimize away.

The boundaries, as always. All ten implementations had FCP between 35 and 71 ms, indistinguishable by feel. Real-site bottlenecks are usually images and video, and framework JS is a small share of total bytes. But the two costs behave differently: media optimization is ongoing labor for the life of the site, while framework weight is fixed forever at pnpm create. AI already flattened the cost of writing the code. For the same feature set, there is no reason to start four times heavier.

First-party numbers, same site, before and after. On Nuxt 4, upweb.dev scored 70/100/100/100 on mobile PageSpeed. I did round after round of tuning at the Cloudflare layer and the performance score would not move; the lab bottleneck kept pointing at JS weight. After the move to SolidStart: 99/100/100/100, simulated Moto G Power on slow 4G, FCP 1.5 s, LCP 1.8 s, CLS 0.006, TBT 0 ms. That zero TBT is where fine-grained reactivity pays off most: there is no virtual tree to reconcile on the main thread.

Squeeze the water out of this comparison before quoting it: Lighthouse lab data, a single-page session, a sample of one site, and the framework was not the only variable. People will remind you that lab scores are not real user experience, and they are right. RUM tells you how users are doing today; the lab tells you how much headroom the architecture leaves. Same domain, same CDN config, framework swapped, and the score went from 70 to 99. Free points. I took them.

Desktop follows the same logic: inside a Tauri WebView, every kilobyte shaved off the runtime comes back as cold-start time and memory.

I did look at Svelte, Vue, Qwik, and Marko

Svelte 5's runes are signals too, and SvelteKit's 54.1 kB is small enough. Vue's reactivity comes from the same family, and Nuxt's 74.7 kB is the best result among the big-three meta-frameworks. What ruled them out is the language layer.

.svelte and .vue are custom file formats. TypeScript is one region inside the file, and type checking runs through a bridge: svelte-language-tools on one side, Volar on the other. The bridge works fine day to day. The bill arrives when the toolchain moves. TypeScript 7.0 went GA on the 8th of this month, a native Go compiler, officially about 10x faster, with type-checking of the VS Code codebase dropping from 125.7 seconds to 10.6. JSX is plain TS, so a Solid project switches the day it ships, zero changes. The compiler API that Vue and Svelte template checking depends on has no stable version in 7.0. They wait for 7.1 in October. Every extra language layer is another place in the upgrade queue.

One more against Nuxt: streaming SSR is still locked behind the experimental ssrStreaming flag, while streaming in SolidStart is one of createHandler's three built-in rendering modes. No flag to flip.

Qwik's resumability solves huge, interaction-sparse landing pages, which my sites are not, and I did not want to carry its mental model. Marko's 28.8 kB is the smallest of the ten, but the ecosystem is too small a bet for two sites I plan to maintain for years.

Reactivity models have been converging. Vue refs, Svelte 5 runes, Angular and Preact signals, plus the TC39 Signals proposal, all point the same way. React is the lone exception, patching around it with a compiler. Signals, JSX, plain TS: ask for all three at once and Solid is what is left.

Astro is smaller than SolidStart. Why not Astro?

One row in that table deserves a straight answer: Astro + HTMX is 34.3 kB, 7 kB smaller than SolidStart. Both of my sites are content-heavy marketing sites. By byte logic alone, Astro wins.

Astro's model is content first, zero JS by default, interactivity added through islands. The catch is that when you write the interactive parts, each island hosts a component framework anyway, and the project now speaks two dialects: .astro templates for page structure, the island framework for behavior, state passed across the seam. And .astro is one more custom file format, so the upgrade-queue problem from the last section applies here too.

Neither site is actually static. keyecho.app runs Stripe checkout, a subscribe flow, and SSR in five languages, with sound-pack sales pages on the way. upweb.dev has quietly grown into a small system: double-opt-in subscriptions, posts and newsletter archives managed in Broadside, and on every publish a webhook hits the site, verifies its signature, dedupes replays, clears the server content cache, then purges Cloudflare by cache tag. New articles go live without a deploy. All of that lives in SolidStart API routes, and the interaction density only goes up from here. One component model covering both the static and the dynamic parts is worth 7 kB to me.

If your site is a pure blog or docs and changes three times a year, Astro is the better answer, not SolidStart. With a bigger team the best practice is to split outright: landing pages on Astro or SolidStart, and the console on its own subdomain with its own stack.

The ecosystem bill: TanStack covers the worst of it, components you grow yourself

SolidStart is not a silver bullet, and its biggest weakness is the ecosystem. Most things React has, Solid has an equivalent for, one maturity tier down and with fewer options. Part of that you tolerate. Part of it has workarounds.

The workaround with the widest coverage is TanStack. Router, Query, Form, Table, Virtual: every package is a core plus adapters, and Solid gets first-class ones. I run the Solid version of TanStack Start in production at difflore.dev: solid-router, solid-query, and oRPC, a full backend with auth, a database, and AI calls, and no missing parts so far. That one vendor crosses off the heaviest items on the list.

The larger trend helps too: infrastructure is going framework-agnostic. Vite, Nitro, Drizzle, Zod, oRPC, better-auth do not care what renders your UI, and headless UI has moved from React-only toward state-machine cores with adapters for everyone. The framework-exclusive share of the ecosystem is shrinking. The flip side deserves saying out loud: every convenience you take from a React-only library raises the wall you would have to climb to ever leave. An ecosystem is an asset and a shackle at the same time.

The gap that remains is the polished component library. On the React side the standard is shadcn/ui; Solid has community ports, at a fraction of the traction. This does not slow me down, and shadcn itself demonstrates why: it ships no npm package. It copies component source into your repo, turning components from a dependency into your own code. Definitions like that are exactly what AI produces fast. One component, written twice, a React version and a Solid version, is minutes of work. None of my current interfaces use a prebuilt library: both sites are hand-written components on top of tokens, the React admin uses Base UI, the Solid side uses Kobalte, all headless primitives with the look and behavior defined by me. AI solved the output problem. What it did not solve is how to design a component API, where state should live, and how tokens get enforced. That skill transfers between React and Solid completely, and the shallower the component-library moat gets, the more it is worth practicing.

Styling: utility classes lost the default slot

The current state first, so this does not read like a takedown: KeyEcho desktop shipped 1.0 on UnoCSS, still runs it today, and I have no plan to rip it out. That UI is small and fixed, there is no design file to match, and the panels rarely change once settled, so utility classes have little room to go wrong there. Admin platforms and marketing sites are a different shape: pages keep multiplying, styles track redesigns, and there is a design language to defend. The two failure modes below belong to that second shape.

Utility classes fail in AI hands at two points.

The first is token drift, and Tailwind and UnoCSS are equally exposed. The project has its own design tokens, and the model still reaches for the default scale in its training memory, or drops in an arbitrary value. gap-3 and gap-[12px] look nearly identical in review, and today they render exactly the same. The difference is that the second one has left the token system, so the next scale change misses it. Every line is legal, and the design system forks a little more with each PR. I have watched this happen more than once. Tailwind v4's @theme cleans up token definitions; arbitrary values remain forever legal.

The second is size. Atomic CSS rests on class reuse: the stylesheet is supposed to stop growing with page count. Enough arbitrary values and that premise collapses. Every w-[347px] is a single-use rule, the stylesheet grows with the PR count, and so do the class strings in the HTML. A constraint model this permissive compiles everything, and the cost gets billed somewhere else.

vanilla-extract turns tokens into TypeScript exports. Styles live in .css.ts files, colors have to be imported from the theme, a misspelled variable is a type error, and a hex value invented from memory does not survive type checking. The theme contract on upweb.dev defines 16 tokens with createThemeContract; light and dark must each assign every key, and one missing dark value fails the build. The rule no longer depends on anyone's memory, or on whether the model read the docs. The output is plain CSS with zero runtime, split by route: a visitor downloads only the styles the current page uses, never a site-wide sheet.

The oldest complaint about CSS-in-TS is naming. AI flipped that one: the model does the naming, I only review, and a named block reads faster than a thirty-character utility string.

The rest of the CSS field

Zero runtime is the floor, which clears the runtime approaches immediately. styled-components and Emotion generate styles in the browser and need extra serialization for SSR, and styled-components is officially in maintenance mode. CSS Modules are zero-runtime, but tokens are string conventions the type system cannot see, so the theme contract is enforced by hoping.

Panda CSS and StyleX are in vanilla-extract's lane: typed, compile-time.

Panda loses on operational shape. It codegens an entire styled-system output directory that must be regenerated whenever tokens change, and the call sites look like a runtime library while behaving like build-time static extraction, so any dynamic object shape (computed keys, spread from a function) gets silently dropped from the stylesheet. Too much pipeline for a content site.

StyleX has nothing wrong with it as a design, and defineVars enforces tokens well, but it is stuck on ecosystem status: two community Vite plugins died in sequence, the official unplugin integration arrived late, and there is no established path in SolidStart. For infrastructure I only pick what the current build chain treats as a first-class citizen. vanilla-extract's official vite-plugin has been stable for years and drops into any Vite-based framework.

Where React still runs

A heavily interactive product I work on at my day job runs React 19 with React Compiler on TanStack Start, and runs well. A team's weight table is different from a solo one: the heavyweight components, editors above all, are written in React, so changing runtimes means a rewrite plus a full regression pass, and QA hours cost more than any framework difference. That alone settled the runtime. At the meta-framework layer they picked TanStack Start over Next.js for equally practical reasons: the product barely touches RSC, so Next.js's architectural complexity buys nothing there, and the Vite toolchain plus fully typed routing came along for free. Loren's table prices that choice at 58 kB: React 19 either way, 118.2 against 176.1, a third of the bytes.

Broadside's admin is a different story. It also runs React with TanStack Start, for a less flattering reason: I needed the platform running and had no time to switch stacks. The plan is to move it onto TanStack Start's Solid adapter, the same combination as difflore.dev: keep the ecosystem, swap the runtime.

Broadside is my self-hosted newsletter platform: an MCP server is built in, an agent drafts, models watch the numbers, and I review and press send. Once it is cleaned up I am open-sourcing it. Separate post then.

Five rules from three decisions

  1. Pick frameworks by client-side bytes. AI erased learning curves from the weight table. Runtime cost lives in the architecture, and no prompt removes it.
  2. A constraint only counts if the compiler enforces it. A rule that fails silently does not exist at AI throughput. Docs get diluted over a long session; type errors do not.
  3. Every extra language layer is another place in the upgrade queue. TS 7 this month: JSX switched on day one, template DSLs wait until October.
  4. Prefer framework-agnostic ecosystems, and grow your own component layer. A single-framework library's convenience and its lock-in come from the same place. Headless primitives plus your own tokens: AI supplies the volume, you supply the rules.
  5. Change the default, do not relitigate the past. UnoCSS still runs the desktop app, and the day-job React product runs fine. New defaults apply to line one of new repos.

upweb.dev and keyecho.app both run on SolidStart + vanilla-extract today. The next repo I open will probably start there too, until some project's shape changes the answer again.

sentences.en: That 130-file PR that shipped KeyEcho 1.0 contained a decision I never wrote about: the desktop app moved from Tauri 1 + Vue to Tauri 2 + SolidJS.
sentences.zh: 发布 KeyEcho 1.0 的 130 个文件 PR 包含一个我从未写过的决定：桌面应用程序从 Tauri 1 + Vue 迁移到 Tauri 2 + SolidJS。

blocks.html_en: The same summer, upweb.dev moved from Nuxt 4 to SolidStart, and keyecho.app (SSR, five languages, Stripe checkout) was built on SolidStart from scratch. Three separate decisions, same answer every time.

sentences.en: The same summer, upweb.dev moved from Nuxt 4 to SolidStart, and keyecho.app (SSR, five languages, Stripe checkout) was built on SolidStart from scratch.
sentences.zh: 同年夏天，upweb.dev 从 Nuxt 4 迁移到 SolidStart，keyecho.app（SSR、五种语言、Stripe checkout）从头开始在 SolidStart 上构建。

sentences.en: Three separate decisions, same answer every time.
sentences.zh: 三个独立的决定，每次都有相同的答案。

blocks.html_en: Scope first. This post is about defaults for my personal projects. Near the end I'll cover two places where I still use React, one of which runs very well.

sentences.en: Scope first.
sentences.zh: 首先是范围。

sentences.en: This post is about defaults for my personal projects.
sentences.zh: 这篇文章是关于我个人项目的默认设置。

sentences.en: Near the end I'll cover two places where I still use React, one of which runs very well.
sentences.zh: 接近尾声时，我将介绍我仍然使用 React 的两个地方，其中之一运行得很好。

blocks.html_en: The criteria changed

sentences.en: The criteria changed
sentences.zh: 标准改变了

blocks.html_en: Most of the code in my repos is AI-generated now. My job has shifted from writing code to reviewing it.

sentences.en: Most of the code in my repos is AI-generated now.
sentences.zh: 我的仓库中的大部分代码现在都是人工智能生成的。

sentences.en: My job has shifted from writing code to reviewing it.
sentences.zh: 我的工作已经从编写代码转变为审查代码。

blocks.html_en: The old framework checklist put most of its weight on learning curve and developer experience. Both are worth zero now: the model writes five frameworks fluently and knows the rules of hooks better than most humans do. AI crushed the price of writing code. Two costs didn't move: the runtime bytes every user downloads, and the human hours spent reviewing. Pick your stack by the new prices.

sentences.en: The old framework checklist put most of its weight on learning curve and developer experience.
sentences.zh: 旧的框架清单主要关注学习曲线和开发人员经验。

sentences.en: Both are worth zero now: the model writes five frameworks fluently and knows the rules of hooks better than most humans do.
sentences.zh: 现在两者的价值都为零：该模型流利地编写了五个框架，并且比大多数人更了解钩子规则。

sentences.en: AI crushed the price of writing code.
sentences.zh: 人工智能降低了编写代码的成本。

sentences.en: Two costs didn't move: the runtime bytes every user downloads, and the human hours spent reviewing.
sentences.zh: 有两项成本没有变化：每个用户下载的运行时字节数，以及审查所花费的人力时间。

sentences.en: Pick your stack by the new prices.
sentences.zh: 根据新价格选择您的堆栈。

blocks.html_en: Runtime cost lives in the architecture. No prompt removes it, so it decides the framework. Review bandwidth is finite, and AI throughput will grind down any consistency that is maintained by verbal agreement, so it decides the styling layer. I have seen more than one React codebase a few years into its life: two animation libraries, two carousel components, three styling systems, each introduced for a perfectly good reason on some ticket, and the sum is a mess nobody can clean up. AI did not invent that drift. It made it an order of magnitude faster.

sentences.en: Runtime cost lives in the architecture.
sentences.zh: 运行时会消耗架构中的生命。

sentences.en: No prompt removes it, so it decides the framework.
sentences.zh: 没有提示可以删除它，因此它决定了框架。

sentences.en: Review bandwidth is finite, and AI throughput will grind down any consistency that is maintained by verbal agreement, so it decides the styling layer.
sentences.zh: 审查带宽是有限的，人工智能吞吐量会降低通过口头协议维持的任何一致性，因此它决定了样式层。

sentences.en: I have seen more than one React codebase a few years into its life: two animation libraries, two carousel components, three styling systems, each introduced for a perfectly good reason on some ticket, and the sum is a mess nobody can clean up.
sentences.zh: 我在几年后见过不止一个 React 代码库：两个动画库、两个轮播组件、三个样式系统，每一个都在某些票据上出于充分的理由而引入，总之就是一团乱，没人能收拾干净。

sentences.en: AI did not invent that drift.
sentences.zh: 人工智能并没有发明这种漂移。

sentences.en: It made it an order of magnitude faster.
sentences.zh: 它使其速度快了一个数量级。

blocks.html_en: React is no longer my default: size and performance

sentences.en: React is no longer my default: size and performance
sentences.zh: React 不再是我的默认设置：大小和性能

blocks.html_en: Let me discard the weak arguments first. Dependency arrays, stale closures, re-render storms: I am not going to relitigate any of it. The model knows the rules of hooks cold, eslint-plugin-react-hooks catches most slips, and React Compiler now handles memoization. The ergonomics debate no longer produces a winner.

sentences.en: Let me discard the weak arguments first.
sentences.zh: 让我先放弃那些薄弱的论点。

sentences.en: Dependency arrays, stale closures, re-render storms: I am not going to relitigate any of it.
sentences.zh: 依赖数组、过时的闭包、重新渲染风暴：我不会对其中任何一个重新提出异议。

sentences.en: The model knows the rules of hooks cold, eslint-plugin-react-hooks catches most slips, and React Compiler now handles memoization.
sentences.zh: 该模型知道 hooks 的规则，eslint-plugin-react-hooks 可以捕获大多数错误，并且 React Compiler 现在可以处理记忆化。

sentences.en: The ergonomics debate no longer produces a winner.
sentences.zh: 人体工程学争论不再产生胜利者。

blocks.html_en: What still produces a winner is the client. react-dom is roughly 40 kB compressed, a virtual DOM plus a reconciler, downloaded, parsed, and executed on every cold visit by every user. React Compiler can remove wasted re-renders. It cannot remove the runtime itself. Developers never feel this cost. Users on phones pay it constantly.

sentences.en: What still produces a winner is the client.
sentences.zh: 仍然能产生赢家的是客户。

sentences.en: react-dom is roughly 40 kB compressed, a virtual DOM plus a reconciler, downloaded, parsed, and executed on every cold visit by every user.
sentences.zh: react-dom 压缩后大约为 40 kB，是一个虚拟 DOM 加上一个协调器，在每个用户的每次冷访问时下载、解析和执行。

sentences.en: React Compiler can remove wasted re-renders.
sentences.zh: React Compiler 可以消除浪费的重新渲染。

sentences.en: It cannot remove the runtime itself.
sentences.zh: 它无法删除运行时本身。

sentences.en: Developers never feel this cost.
sentences.zh: 开发者从来感受不到这个成本。

sentences.en: Users on phones pay it constantly.
sentences.zh: 手机用户不断付费。

blocks.html_en: My projects are one person plus a fleet of agents. Hiring pools do not help me, and content sites barely touch the component-library ecosystem. Once the weights shift like that, React has no slot left in my personal stack.

sentences.en: My projects are one person plus a fleet of agents.
sentences.zh: 我的项目是一个人加上一群代理。

sentences.en: Hiring pools do not help me, and content sites barely touch the component-library ecosystem.
sentences.zh: 招聘池对我没有帮助，内容网站几乎没有触及组件库生态系统。

sentences.en: Once the weights shift like that, React has no slot left in my personal stack.
sentences.zh: 一旦权重发生这样的变化，React 在我的个人堆栈中就没有剩余位置了。

blocks.html_en: The same app, written ten times

sentences.en: The same app, written ten times
sentences.zh: 同一个应用程序，写了十次

blocks.html_en: Loren Stewart ran an experiment last year: the same kanban app implemented in ten frameworks, identical features (drag and drop, comments, tags, server validation), then compared compressed JS on the board page.

sentences.en: Loren Stewart ran an experiment last year: the same kanban app implemented in ten frameworks, identical features (drag and drop, comments, tags, server validation), then compared compressed JS on the board page.
sentences.zh: Loren Stewart 去年进行了一项实验：在十个框架中实现相同的看板应用程序，具有相同的功能（拖放、评论、标签、服务器验证），然后比较看板页面上的压缩 JS。

blocks.html_en: Framework

sentences.en: Framework
sentences.zh: 框架

blocks.html_en: Compressed JS

sentences.en: Compressed JS
sentences.zh: 压缩JS

sentences.en: Next.js 16 (React 19)
sentences.zh: Next.js 16（反应 19）

sentences.en: TanStack Start (React 19)
sentences.zh: TanStack 启动 (React 19)

sentences.en: Analog (Angular 20)
sentences.zh: 模拟（角度 20）

Two comparisons stand out. Same meta-framework, TanStack Start, with React swapped for Solid: 118.2 kB becomes 60.4 kB. Next.js against SolidStart: a 4.2x gap. His conclusion was that React's architectural cost is something no meta-framework can optimize away.

sentences.en: Two comparisons stand out.
sentences.zh: 有两个比较很突出。

sentences.en: Same meta-framework, TanStack Start, with React swapped for Solid: 118.2 kB becomes 60.4 kB.
sentences.zh: 相同的元框架 TanStack Start，将 React 替换为 Solid：118.2 kB 变为 60.4 kB。

sentences.en: Next.js against SolidStart: a 4.2x gap.
sentences.zh: Next.js 与 SolidStart：4.2 倍的差距。

sentences.en: His conclusion was that React's architectural cost is something no meta-framework can optimize away.
sentences.zh: 他的结论是，React 的架构成本是任何元框架都无法优化掉的。

blocks.html_en: The boundaries, as always. All ten implementations had FCP between 35 and 71 ms, indistinguishable by feel. Real-site bottlenecks are usually images and video, and framework JS is a small share of total bytes. But the two costs behave differently: media optimization is ongoing labor for the life of the site, while framework weight is fixed forever at pnpm create. AI already flattened the cost of writing the code. For the same feature set, there is no reason to start four times heavier.

sentences.en: The boundaries, as always.
sentences.zh: 边界，一如既往。

sentences.en: All ten implementations had FCP between 35 and 71 ms, indistinguishable by feel.
sentences.zh: 所有十个实现的 FCP 都在 35 到 71 毫秒之间，凭感觉无法区分。

sentences.en: Real-site bottlenecks are usually images and video, and framework JS is a small share of total bytes.
sentences.zh: 实际站点的瓶颈通常是图像和视频，框架 JS 只占总字节数的一小部分。

sentences.en: But the two costs behave differently: media optimization is ongoing labor for the life of the site, while framework weight is fixed forever at pnpm create.
sentences.zh: 但这两种成本的表现不同：媒体优化是在网站生命周期内持续进行的工作，而框架权重则永远固定在 pnpm create 上。

sentences.en: AI already flattened the cost of writing the code.
sentences.zh: 人工智能已经降低了编写代码的成本。

sentences.en: For the same feature set, there is no reason to start four times heavier.
sentences.zh: 对于相同的功能集，没有理由一开始就重四倍。

blocks.html_en: First-party numbers, same site, before and after. On Nuxt 4, upweb.dev scored 70/100/100/100 on mobile PageSpeed. I did round after round of tuning at the Cloudflare layer and the performance score would not move; the lab bottleneck kept pointing at JS weight. After the move to SolidStart: 99/100/100/100, simulated Moto G Power on slow 4G, FCP 1.5 s, LCP 1.8 s, CLS 0.006, TBT 0 ms. That zero TBT is where fine-grained reactivity pays off most: there is no virtual tree to reconcile on the main thread.

sentences.en: First-party numbers, same site, before and after.
sentences.zh: 第一方号码、同一站点、之前和之后。

sentences.en: On Nuxt 4, upweb.dev scored 70/100/100/100 on mobile PageSpeed.
sentences.zh: 在 Nuxt 4 上，upweb.dev 在移动 PageSpeed 上的得分为 70/100/100/100。

sentences.en: I did round after round of tuning at the Cloudflare layer and the performance score would not move; the lab bottleneck kept pointing at JS weight.
sentences.zh: 我在Cloudflare层做了一轮又一轮的调优，性能分数没有变化；实验室的瓶颈一直指向 JS 的重量。

sentences.en: After the move to SolidStart: 99/100/100/100, simulated Moto G Power on slow 4G, FCP 1.5 s, LCP 1.8 s, CLS 0.006, TBT 0 ms.
sentences.zh: 转移到 SolidStart 后：99/100/100/100，模拟 Moto G Power 慢速 4G、FCP 1.5 秒、LCP 1.8 秒、CLS 0.006、TBT 0 毫秒。

sentences.en: That zero TBT is where fine-grained reactivity pays off most: there is no virtual tree to reconcile on the main thread.
sentences.zh: 零 TBT 是细粒度反应性最能带来回报的地方：主线程上没有需要协调的虚拟树。

blocks.html_en: Squeeze the water out of this comparison before quoting it: Lighthouse lab data, a single-page session, a sample of one site, and the framework was not the only variable. People will remind you that lab scores are not real user experience, and they are right. RUM tells you how users are doing today; the lab tells you how much headroom the architecture leaves. Same domain, same CDN config, framework swapped, and the score went from 70 to 99. Free points. I took them.

sentences.en: Squeeze the water out of this comparison before quoting it: Lighthouse lab data, a single-page session, a sample of one site, and the framework was not the only variable.
sentences.zh: 在引用之前先从这个比较中挤出水分：灯塔实验室数据、单页会话、一个站点的样本，并且框架并不是唯一的变量。

sentences.en: People will remind you that lab scores are not real user experience, and they are right.
sentences.zh: 人们会提醒你，实验室分数并不是真正的用户体验，他们是对的。

sentences.en: RUM tells you how users are doing today; the lab tells you how much headroom the architecture leaves.
sentences.zh: RUM 告诉您用户今天的表现如何；实验室会告诉您该架构还剩下多少空间。

sentences.en: Same domain, same CDN config, framework swapped, and the score went from 70 to 99.
sentences.zh: 相同的域名，相同的 CDN 配置，交换了框架，分数从 70 变成了 99。

sentences.en: Free points.
sentences.zh: 免费积分。

sentences.en: I took them.
sentences.zh: 我拿走了它们。

blocks.html_en: Desktop follows the same logic: inside a Tauri WebView, every kilobyte shaved off the runtime comes back as cold-start time and memory.

sentences.en: Desktop follows the same logic: inside a Tauri WebView, every kilobyte shaved off the runtime comes back as cold-start time and memory.
sentences.zh: 桌面遵循相同的逻辑：在 Tauri WebView 内，运行时减少的每一千字节都会作为冷启动时间和内存返回。

blocks.html_en: I did look at Svelte, Vue, Qwik, and Marko

sentences.en: I did look at Svelte, Vue, Qwik, and Marko
sentences.zh: 我确实看过 Svelte、Vue、Qwik 和 Marko

blocks.html_en: Svelte 5's runes are signals too, and SvelteKit's 54.1 kB is small enough. Vue's reactivity comes from the same family, and Nuxt's 74.7 kB is the best result among the big-three meta-frameworks. What ruled them out is the language layer.

sentences.en: Svelte 5's runes are signals too, and SvelteKit's 54.1 kB is small enough.
sentences.zh: Svelte 5 的符文也是信号，SvelteKit 的 54.1 kB 已经足够小了。

sentences.en: Vue's reactivity comes from the same family, and Nuxt's 74.7 kB is the best result among the big-three meta-frameworks.
sentences.zh: Vue 的反应性来自同一个家族，Nuxt 的 74.7 kB 是三大元框架中最好的结果。

sentences.en: What ruled them out is the language layer.
sentences.zh: 排除它们的是语言层。

blocks.html_en: .svelte and .vue are custom file formats. TypeScript is one region inside the file, and type checking runs through a bridge: svelte-language-tools on one side, Volar on the other. The bridge works fine day to day. The bill arrives when the toolchain moves. TypeScript 7.0 went GA on the 8th of this month, a native Go compiler, officially about 10x faster, with type-checking of the VS Code codebase dropping from 125.7 seconds to 10.6. JSX is plain TS, so a Solid project switches the day it ships, zero changes. The compiler API that Vue and Svelte template checking depends on has no stable version in 7.0. They wait for 7.1 in October. Every extra language layer is another place in the upgrade queue.

sentences.en: .svelte and .vue are custom file formats.
sentences.zh: .svelte 和 .vue 是自定义文件格式。

sentences.en: TypeScript is one region inside the file, and type checking runs through a bridge: svelte-language-tools on one side, Volar on the other.
sentences.zh: TypeScript 是文件内的一个区域，类型检查通过一座桥运行：一侧是 svelte-language-tools，另一侧是 Volar。

sentences.en: The bridge works fine day to day.
sentences.zh: 这座桥每天都运转良好。

sentences.en: The bill arrives when the toolchain moves.
sentences.zh: 当工具链移动时，账单就会到达。

sentences.en: TypeScript 7.0 went GA on the 8th of this month, a native Go compiler, officially about 10x faster, with type-checking of the VS Code codebase dropping from 125.7 seconds to 10.6.
sentences.zh: TypeScript 7.0 于本月 8 日发布 GA，这是一个原生 Go 编译器，官方公布速度提高了 10 倍左右，VS Code 代码库的类型检查从 125.7 秒降至 10.6 秒。

sentences.en: JSX is plain TS, so a Solid project switches the day it ships, zero changes.
sentences.zh: JSX 是普通的 TS，因此 Solid 项目会在发布之日进行切换，零更改。

sentences.en: The compiler API that Vue and Svelte template checking depends on has no stable version in 7.0.
sentences.zh: Vue和Svelte模板检查所依赖的编译器API在7.0中没有稳定版本。

sentences.en: They wait for 7.1 in October.
sentences.zh: 他们等待 10 月的 7.1。

sentences.en: Every extra language layer is another place in the upgrade queue.
sentences.zh: 每个额外的语言层都是升级队列中的另一个位置。

blocks.html_en: One more against Nuxt: streaming SSR is still locked behind the experimental ssrStreaming flag, while streaming in SolidStart is one of createHandler's three built-in rendering modes. No flag to flip.

sentences.en: One more against Nuxt: streaming SSR is still locked behind the experimental ssrStreaming flag, while streaming in SolidStart is one of createHandler's three built-in rendering modes.
sentences.zh: 还有一个针对 Nuxt 的问题：流式 SSR 仍然被锁定在实验性 ssrStreaming 标志后面，而 SolidStart 中的流式传输是 createHandler 的三种内置渲染模式之一。

sentences.en: No flag to flip.
sentences.zh: 没有旗帜可以翻转。

blocks.html_en: Qwik's resumability solves huge, interaction-sparse landing pages, which my sites are not, and I did not want to carry its mental model. Marko's 28.8 kB is the smallest of the ten, but the ecosystem is too small a bet for two sites I plan to maintain for years.

sentences.en: Qwik's resumability solves huge, interaction-sparse landing pages, which my sites are not, and I did not want to carry its mental model.
sentences.zh: Qwik 的可恢复性解决了巨大的、交互稀疏的登陆页面的问题，而我的网站则没有，而且我不想采用它的思维模型。

sentences.en: Marko's 28.8 kB is the smallest of the ten, but the ecosystem is too small a bet for two sites I plan to maintain for years.
sentences.zh: Marko 的 28.8 kB 是十个网站中最小的，但对于我计划维护多年的两个网站来说，这个生态系统太小了。

blocks.html_en: Reactivity models have been converging. Vue refs, Svelte 5 runes, Angular and Preact signals, plus the TC39 Signals proposal, all point the same way. React is the lone exception, patching around it with a compiler. Signals, JSX, plain TS: ask for all three at once and Solid is what is left.

sentences.en: Reactivity models have been converging.
sentences.zh: 反应模型已经趋同。

sentences.en: Vue refs, Svelte 5 runes, Angular and Preact signals, plus the TC39 Signals proposal, all point the same way.
sentences.zh: Vue refs、Svelte 5 runes、Angular 和 Preact 信号，加上 TC39 Signals 提案，都指向相同的方向。

sentences.en: React is the lone exception, patching around it with a compiler.
sentences.zh: React 是唯一的例外，它通过编译器进行修补。

sentences.en: Signals, JSX, plain TS: ask for all three at once and Solid is what is left.
sentences.zh: Signals、JSX、plain TS：同时要求这三个，剩下的就是 Solid。

blocks.html_en: Astro is smaller than SolidStart. Why not Astro?

sentences.en: Astro is smaller than SolidStart.
sentences.zh: Astro 比 SolidStart 小。

sentences.en: Why not Astro?
sentences.zh: 为什么不是阿斯特罗？

blocks.html_en: One row in that table deserves a straight answer: Astro + HTMX is 34.3 kB, 7 kB smaller than SolidStart. Both of my sites are content-heavy marketing sites. By byte logic alone, Astro wins.

sentences.en: One row in that table deserves a straight answer: Astro + HTMX is 34.3 kB, 7 kB smaller than SolidStart.
sentences.zh: 该表中的一行值得直接回答：Astro + HTMX 为 34.3 kB，比 SolidStart 小 7 kB。

sentences.en: Both of my sites are content-heavy marketing sites.
sentences.zh: 我的两个网站都是内容丰富的营销网站。

sentences.en: By byte logic alone, Astro wins.
sentences.zh: 仅凭字节逻辑，Astro 就获胜了。

blocks.html_en: Astro's model is content first, zero JS by default, interactivity added through islands. The catch is that when you write the interactive parts, each island hosts a component framework anyway, and the project now speaks two dialects: .astro templates for page structure, the island framework for behavior, state passed across the seam. And .astro is one more custom file format, so the upgrade-queue problem from the last section applies here too.

sentences.en: Astro's model is content first, zero JS by default, interactivity added through islands.
sentences.zh: Astro的模型是内容优先，默认零JS，通过岛屿添加交互性。

sentences.en: The catch is that when you write the interactive parts, each island hosts a component framework anyway, and the project now speaks two dialects: .astro templates for page structure, the island framework for behavior, state passed across the seam.
sentences.zh: 问题是，当您编写交互部分时，每个岛都会托管一个组件框架，并且该项目现在讲两种方言：用于页面结构的 .astro 模板、用于行为的岛框架、跨接缝传递的状态。

sentences.en: And .astro is one more custom file format, so the upgrade-queue problem from the last section applies here too.
sentences.zh: .astro 是另一种自定义文件格式，因此上一节中的升级队列问题也适用于此。

blocks.html_en: Neither site is actually static. keyecho.app runs Stripe checkout, a subscribe flow, and SSR in five languages, with sound-pack sales pages on the way. upweb.dev has quietly grown into a small system: double-opt-in subscriptions, posts and newsletter archives managed in Broadside, and on every publish a webhook hits the site, verifies its signature, dedupes replays, clears the server content cache, then purges Cloudflare by cache tag. New articles go live without a deploy. All of that lives in SolidStart API routes, and the interaction density only goes up from here. One component model covering both the static and the dynamic parts is worth 7 kB to me.

sentences.en: Neither site is actually static.
sentences.zh: 这两个网站实际上都不是静态的。

sentences.en: keyecho.app runs Stripe checkout, a subscribe flow, and SSR in five languages, with sound-pack sales pages on the way.
sentences.zh: keyecho.app 运行 Stripe 结帐、订阅流程和五种语言的 SSR，并且即将推出声音包销售页面。

sentences.en: upweb.dev has quietly grown into a small system: double-opt-in subscriptions, posts and newsletter archives managed in Broadside, and on every publish a webhook hits the site, verifies its signature, dedupes replays, clears the server content cache, then purges Cloudflare by cache tag.
sentences.zh: upweb.dev 已悄然成长为一个小型系统：双重选择加入订阅、帖子和时事通讯档案均在 Broadside 中管理，每次发布时都会有一个 Webhook 访问该网站，验证其签名、重复数据删除、清除服务器内容缓存，然后通过缓存标签清除 Cloudflare。

sentences.en: New articles go live without a deploy.
sentences.zh: 新文章无需部署即可上线。

sentences.en: All of that lives in SolidStart API routes, and the interaction density only goes up from here.
sentences.zh: 所有这些都存在于 SolidStart API 路由中，交互密度从此只会上升。

sentences.en: One component model covering both the static and the dynamic parts is worth 7 kB to me.
sentences.zh: 一个涵盖静态和动态部分的组件模型对我来说价值 7 kB。

blocks.html_en: If your site is a pure blog or docs and changes three times a year, Astro is the better answer, not SolidStart. With a bigger team the best practice is to split outright: landing pages on Astro or SolidStart, and the console on its own subdomain with its own stack.

sentences.en: If your site is a pure blog or docs and changes three times a year, Astro is the better answer, not SolidStart.
sentences.zh: 如果您的网站是纯粹的博客或文档，并且每年更改 3 次，那么 Astro 是更好的答案，而不是 SolidStart。

sentences.en: With a bigger team the best practice is to split outright: landing pages on Astro or SolidStart, and the console on its own subdomain with its own stack.
sentences.zh: 对于更大的团队，最佳实践是彻底拆分：Astro 或 SolidStart 上的登录页面，以及具有自己堆栈的其自己的子域上的控制台。

blocks.html_en: The ecosystem bill: TanStack covers the worst of it, components you grow yourself

sentences.en: The ecosystem bill: TanStack covers the worst of it, components you grow yourself
sentences.zh: 生态系统法案：TanStack 涵盖了其中最糟糕的部分，即您自己开发的组件

blocks.html_en: SolidStart is not a silver bullet, and its biggest weakness is the ecosystem. Most things React has, Solid has an equivalent for, one maturity tier down and with fewer options. Part of that you tolerate. Part of it has workarounds.

sentences.en: SolidStart is not a silver bullet, and its biggest weakness is the ecosystem.
sentences.zh: SolidStart 并不是灵丹妙药，它最大的弱点是生态系统。

sentences.en: Most things React has, Solid has an equivalent for, one maturity tier down and with fewer options.
sentences.zh: React 拥有的大多数东西，Solid 都有一个等价的东西，成熟度降低了一层，选择也更少。

sentences.en: Part of that you tolerate.
sentences.zh: 你可以忍受其中的一部分。

sentences.en: Part of it has workarounds.
sentences.zh: 其中一部分有解决方法。

blocks.html_en: The workaround with the widest coverage is TanStack. Router, Query, Form, Table, Virtual: every package is a core plus adapters, and Solid gets first-class ones. I run the Solid version of TanStack Start in production at difflore.dev: solid-router, solid-query, and oRPC, a full backend with auth, a database, and AI calls, and no missing parts so far. That one vendor crosses off the heaviest items on the list.

sentences.en: The workaround with the widest coverage is TanStack.
sentences.zh: 覆盖范围最广的解决方法是 TanStack。

sentences.en: Router, Query, Form, Table, Virtual: every package is a core plus adapters, and Solid gets first-class ones.
sentences.zh: Router、Query、Form、Table、Virtual：每个包都是一个核心加上适配器，Solid 是一流的。

sentences.en: I run the Solid version of TanStack Start in production at difflore.dev: solid-router, solid-query, and oRPC, a full backend with auth, a database, and AI calls, and no missing parts so far.
sentences.zh: 我在 difflore.dev 的生产环境中运行 Solid 版本的 TanStack Start：solid-router、solid-query 和 oRPC，一个带有身份验证、数据库和 AI 调用的完整后端，到目前为止没有丢失任何部件。

sentences.en: That one vendor crosses off the heaviest items on the list.
sentences.zh: 该供应商划掉了清单上最重的物品。

blocks.html_en: The larger trend helps too: infrastructure is going framework-agnostic. Vite, Nitro, Drizzle, Zod, oRPC, better-auth do not care what renders your UI, and headless UI has moved from React-only toward state-machine cores with adapters for everyone. The framework-exclusive share of the ecosystem is shrinking. The flip side deserves saying out loud: every convenience you take from a React-only library raises the wall you would have to climb to ever leave. An ecosystem is an asset and a shackle at the same time.

sentences.en: The larger trend helps too: infrastructure is going framework-agnostic.
sentences.zh: 更大的趋势也有所帮助：基础设施正在变得与框架无关。

sentences.en: Vite, Nitro, Drizzle, Zod, oRPC, better-auth do not care what renders your UI, and headless UI has moved from React-only toward state-machine cores with adapters for everyone.
sentences.zh: Vite、Nitro、Drizzle、Zod、oRPC、better-auth 并不关心你的 UI 的渲染内容，无头 UI 已经从 React-only 转向带有适合所有人的适配器的状态机核心。

sentences.en: The framework-exclusive share of the ecosystem is shrinking.
sentences.zh: 生态系统中框架独有的份额正在缩小。

sentences.en: The flip side deserves saying out loud: every convenience you take from a React-only library raises the wall you would have to climb to ever leave.
sentences.zh: 另一方面值得大声说出来：你从仅限 React 的库中获得的每一项便利都会增加你必须爬过才能离开的墙。

sentences.en: An ecosystem is an asset and a shackle at the same time.
sentences.zh: 生态系统既是资产，又是枷锁。

blocks.html_en: The gap that remains is the polished component library. On the React side the standard is shadcn/ui; Solid has community ports, at a fraction of the traction. This does not slow me down, and shadcn itself demonstrates why: it ships no npm package. It copies component source into your repo, turning components from a dependency into your own code. Definitions like that are exactly what AI produces fast. One component, written twice, a React version and a Solid version, is minutes of work. None of my current interfaces use a prebuilt library: both sites are hand-written components on top of tokens, the React admin uses Base UI, the Solid side uses Kobalte, all headless primitives with the look and behavior defined by me. AI solved the output problem. What it did not solve is how to design a component API, where state should live, and how tokens get enforced. That skill transfers between React and Solid completely, and the shallower the component-library moat gets, the more it is worth practicing.

sentences.en: The gap that remains is the polished component library.
sentences.zh: 剩下的差距是完善的组件库。

sentences.en: On the React side the standard is shadcn/ui; Solid has community ports, at a fraction of the traction.
sentences.zh: React 方面的标准是 shadcn/ui； Solid 有社区端口，但牵引力只是其中的一小部分。

sentences.en: This does not slow me down, and shadcn itself demonstrates why: it ships no npm package.
sentences.zh: 这并没有让我放慢速度，shadcn 本身就说明了原因：它不附带 npm 包。

sentences.en: It copies component source into your repo, turning components from a dependency into your own code.
sentences.zh: 它将组件源复制到您的存储库中，将组件从依赖项转换为您自己的代码。

sentences.en: Definitions like that are exactly what AI produces fast.
sentences.zh: 像这样的定义正是人工智能快速产生的。

sentences.en: One component, written twice, a React version and a Solid version, is minutes of work.
sentences.zh: 一个组件写了两次，一个 React 版本和一个 Solid 版本，需要几分钟的时间。

sentences.en: None of my current interfaces use a prebuilt library: both sites are hand-written components on top of tokens, the React admin uses Base UI, the Solid side uses Kobalte, all headless primitives with the look and behavior defined by me.
sentences.zh: 我当前的界面都没有使用预构建的库：两个站点都是在 token 之上手写的组件，React 管理员使用 Base UI，Solid 端使用 Kobalte，所有无头原语的外观和行为都由我定义。

sentences.en: AI solved the output problem.
sentences.zh: AI解决了输出问题。

sentences.en: What it did not solve is how to design a component API, where state should live, and how tokens get enforced.
sentences.zh: 它没有解决的是如何设计组件 API、状态应该存在于何处以及如何强制执行令牌。

sentences.en: That skill transfers between React and Solid completely, and the shallower the component-library moat gets, the more it is worth practicing.
sentences.zh: 该技能可以在 React 和 Solid 之间完全转移，并且组件库护城河越浅，就越值得练习。

blocks.html_en: Styling: utility classes lost the default slot

sentences.en: Styling: utility classes lost the default slot
sentences.zh: 样式：实用程序类失去了默认插槽

blocks.html_en: The current state first, so this does not read like a takedown: KeyEcho desktop shipped 1.0 on UnoCSS, still runs it today, and I have no plan to rip it out. That UI is small and fixed, there is no design file to match, and the panels rarely change once settled, so utility classes have little room to go wrong there. Admin platforms and marketing sites are a different shape: pages keep multiplying, styles track redesigns, and there is a design language to defend. The two failure modes below belong to that second shape.

sentences.en: The current state first, so this does not read like a takedown: KeyEcho desktop shipped 1.0 on UnoCSS, still runs it today, and I have no plan to rip it out.
sentences.zh: 首先是当前状态，所以这读起来不像是删除：KeyEcho 桌面版在 UnoCSS 上发布了 1.0，今天仍在运行，而且我没有计划将其删除。

sentences.en: That UI is small and fixed, there is no design file to match, and the panels rarely change once settled, so utility classes have little room to go wrong there.
sentences.zh: 该 UI 很小且固定，没有可匹配的设计文件，并且面板一旦确定就很少更改，因此实用程序类在那里几乎没有出错的空间。

sentences.en: Admin platforms and marketing sites are a different shape: pages keep multiplying, styles track redesigns, and there is a design language to defend.
sentences.zh: 管理平台和营销网站的形状不同：页面不断增加，样式跟踪重新设计，并且有一种设计语言需要捍卫。

sentences.en: The two failure modes below belong to that second shape.
sentences.zh: 下面的两种失效模式属于第二种形状。

blocks.html_en: Utility classes fail in AI hands at two points.

sentences.en: Utility classes fail in AI hands at two points.
sentences.zh: 实用程序类在人工智能手中失败有两点。

blocks.html_en: The first is token drift, and Tailwind and UnoCSS are equally exposed. The project has its own design tokens, and the model still reaches for the default scale in its training memory, or drops in an arbitrary value. gap-3 and gap-[12px] look nearly identical in review, and today they render exactly the same. The difference is that the second one has left the token system, so the next scale change misses it. Every line is legal, and the design system forks a little more with each PR. I have watched this happen more than once. Tailwind v4's @theme cleans up token definitions; arbitrary values remain forever legal.

sentences.en: The first is token drift, and Tailwind and UnoCSS are equally exposed.
sentences.zh: 首先是token漂移，Tailwind和UnoCSS同样暴露。

sentences.en: The project has its own design tokens, and the model still reaches for the default scale in its training memory, or drops in an arbitrary value.
sentences.zh: 该项目有自己的设计标记，模型仍然达到其训练内存中的默认比例，或者下降到任意值。

sentences.en: gap-3 and gap-[12px] look nearly identical in review, and today they render exactly the same.
sentences.zh: 在审查中，gap-3 和gap-[12px] 看起来几乎相同，今天它们的渲染效果完全相同。

sentences.en: The difference is that the second one has left the token system, so the next scale change misses it.
sentences.zh: 不同的是，第二个已经离开了代币系统，因此下一次规模变更会错过它。

sentences.en: Every line is legal, and the design system forks a little more with each PR.
sentences.zh: 每条线都是合法的，并且设计系统为每个 PR 分叉更多一点。

sentences.en: I have watched this happen more than once.
sentences.zh: 我不止一次看到这种情况发生。

sentences.en: Tailwind v4's @theme cleans up token definitions; arbitrary values remain forever legal.
sentences.zh: Tailwind v4 的 @theme 清理了 token 定义；任意值永远合法。

blocks.html_en: The second is size. Atomic CSS rests on class reuse: the stylesheet is supposed to stop growing with page count. Enough arbitrary values and that premise collapses. Every w-[347px] is a single-use rule, the stylesheet grows with the PR count, and so do the class strings in the HTML. A constraint model this permissive compiles everything, and the cost gets billed somewhere else.

sentences.en: The second is size.
sentences.zh: 第二是尺寸。

sentences.en: Atomic CSS rests on class reuse: the stylesheet is supposed to stop growing with page count.
sentences.zh: 原子 CSS 依赖于类重用：样式表应该随着页数的增加而停止增长。

sentences.en: Enough arbitrary values and that premise collapses.
sentences.zh: 任意值太多了，这个前提就崩溃了。

sentences.en: Every w-[347px] is a single-use rule, the stylesheet grows with the PR count, and so do the class strings in the HTML.
sentences.zh: 每个 w-[347px] 都是一次性规则，样式表随着 PR 计数而增长，HTML 中的类字符串也是如此。

sentences.en: A constraint model this permissive compiles everything, and the cost gets billed somewhere else.
sentences.zh: 这种宽松的约束模型可以编译所有内容，而成本则在其他地方计费。

blocks.html_en: vanilla-extract turns tokens into TypeScript exports. Styles live in .css.ts files, colors have to be imported from the theme, a misspelled variable is a type error, and a hex value invented from memory does not survive type checking. The theme contract on upweb.dev defines 16 tokens with createThemeContract; light and dark must each assign every key, and one missing dark value fails the build. The rule no longer depends on anyone's memory, or on whether the model read the docs. The output is plain CSS with zero runtime, split by route: a visitor downloads only the styles the current page uses, never a site-wide sheet.

sentences.en: vanilla-extract turns tokens into TypeScript exports.
sentences.zh: vanilla-extract 将 token 转换为 TypeScript 导出。

sentences.en: Styles live in .css.ts files, colors have to be imported from the theme, a misspelled variable is a type error, and a hex value invented from memory does not survive type checking.
sentences.zh: 样式位于 .css.ts 文件中，颜色必须从主题导入，拼写错误的变量是类型错误，从内存中发明的十六进制值无法通过类型检查。

sentences.en: The theme contract on upweb.dev defines 16 tokens with createThemeContract; light and dark must each assign every key, and one missing dark value fails the build.
sentences.zh: upweb.dev 上的主题合约通过 createThemeContract 定义了 16 个代币；浅色和深色必须分别分配每个键，并且缺少一个深色值会导致构建失败。

sentences.en: The rule no longer depends on anyone's memory, or on whether the model read the docs.
sentences.zh: 该规则不再取决于任何人的记忆，或模型是否阅读文档。

sentences.en: The output is plain CSS with zero runtime, split by route: a visitor downloads only the styles the current page uses, never a site-wide sheet.
sentences.zh: 输出是纯 CSS，运行时间为零，按路线分割：访问者仅下载当前页面使用的样式，而不是站点范围的工作表。

blocks.html_en: The oldest complaint about CSS-in-TS is naming. AI flipped that one: the model does the naming, I only review, and a named block reads faster than a thirty-character utility string.

sentences.en: The oldest complaint about CSS-in-TS is naming.
sentences.zh: 关于 CSS-in-TS 最古老的抱怨是命名。

sentences.en: AI flipped that one: the model does the naming, I only review, and a named block reads faster than a thirty-character utility string.
sentences.zh: 人工智能翻转了这一点：模型进行命名，我只进行审查，并且命名块的读取速度比三十个字符的实用字符串更快。

blocks.html_en: The rest of the CSS field

sentences.en: The rest of the CSS field
sentences.zh: CSS 字段的其余部分

blocks.html_en: Zero runtime is the floor, which clears the runtime approaches immediately. styled-components and Emotion generate styles in the browser and need extra serialization for SSR, and styled-components is officially in maintenance mode. CSS Modules are zero-runtime, but tokens are string conventions the type system cannot see, so the theme contract is enforced by hoping.

sentences.en: Zero runtime is the floor, which clears the runtime approaches immediately.
sentences.zh: 零运行时间是下限，它立即清除运行时间逼近。

sentences.en: styled-components and Emotion generate styles in the browser and need extra serialization for SSR, and styled-components is officially in maintenance mode.
sentences.zh: styled-components 和 Emotion 在浏览器中生成样式，需要额外的 SSR 序列化，并且 styled-components 正式处于维护模式。

sentences.en: CSS Modules are zero-runtime, but tokens are string conventions the type system cannot see, so the theme contract is enforced by hoping.
sentences.zh: CSS 模块是零运行时的，但标记是类型系统看不到的字符串约定，因此主题契约是通过希望来强制执行的。

blocks.html_en: Panda CSS and StyleX are in vanilla-extract's lane: typed, compile-time.

sentences.en: Panda CSS and StyleX are in vanilla-extract's lane: typed, compile-time.
sentences.zh: Panda CSS 和 StyleX 属于 vanilla-extract 的范畴：类型化、编译时。

blocks.html_en: Panda loses on operational shape. It codegens an entire styled-system output directory that must be regenerated whenever tokens change, and the call sites look like a runtime library while behaving like build-time static extraction, so any dynamic object shape (computed keys, spread from a function) gets silently dropped from the stylesheet. Too much pipeline for a content site.

sentences.en: Panda loses on operational shape.
sentences.zh: 熊猫失去了运营形态。

sentences.en: It codegens an entire styled-system output directory that must be regenerated whenever tokens change, and the call sites look like a runtime library while behaving like build-time static extraction, so any dynamic object shape (computed keys, spread from a function) gets silently dropped from the stylesheet.
sentences.zh: 它代码生成了整个样式系统输出目录，每当标记发生更改时都必须重新生成该目录，并且调用站点看起来像一个运行时库，同时表现得像构建时静态提取，因此任何动态对象形状（计算的键，从函数传播）都会从样式表中悄悄删除。

sentences.en: Too much pipeline for a content site.
sentences.zh: 内容网站的管道太多。

blocks.html_en: StyleX has nothing wrong with it as a design, and defineVars enforces tokens well, but it is stuck on ecosystem status: two community Vite plugins died in sequence, the official unplugin integration arrived late, and there is no established path in SolidStart. For infrastructure I only pick what the current build chain treats as a first-class citizen. vanilla-extract's official vite-plugin has been stable for years and drops into any Vite-based framework.

sentences.en: StyleX has nothing wrong with it as a design, and defineVars enforces tokens well, but it is stuck on ecosystem status: two community Vite plugins died in sequence, the official unplugin integration arrived late, and there is no established path in SolidStart.
sentences.zh: StyleX 的设计没有任何问题，defineVars 强制执行代币也很好，但它陷入了生态系统状态：两个社区 Vite 插件相继死亡，官方的 unplugin 集成迟到，SolidStart 中没有既定路径。

sentences.en: For infrastructure I only pick what the current build chain treats as a first-class citizen.
sentences.zh: 对于基础设施，我只选择当前构建链视为一等公民的基础设施。

sentences.en: vanilla-extract's official vite-plugin has been stable for years and drops into any Vite-based framework.
sentences.zh: vanilla-extract 的官方 vite 插件多年来一直保持稳定，并且可以嵌入到任何基于 Vite 的框架中。

blocks.html_en: Where React still runs

sentences.en: Where React still runs
sentences.zh: React 仍然运行的地方

blocks.html_en: A heavily interactive product I work on at my day job runs React 19 with React Compiler on TanStack Start, and runs well. A team's weight table is different from a solo one: the heavyweight components, editors above all, are written in React, so changing runtimes means a rewrite plus a full regression pass, and QA hours cost more than any framework difference. That alone settled the runtime. At the meta-framework layer they picked TanStack Start over Next.js for equally practical reasons: the product barely touches RSC, so Next.js's architectural complexity buys nothing there, and the Vite toolchain plus fully typed routing came along for free. Loren's table prices that choice at 58 kB: React 19 either way, 118.2 against 176.1, a third of the bytes.

sentences.en: A heavily interactive product I work on at my day job runs React 19 with React Compiler on TanStack Start, and runs well.
sentences.zh: 我日常工作中开发的一个高度交互的产品在 TanStack Start 上运行 React 19 和 React Compiler，并且运行良好。

sentences.en: A team's weight table is different from a solo one: the heavyweight components, editors above all, are written in React, so changing runtimes means a rewrite plus a full regression pass, and QA hours cost more than any framework difference.
sentences.zh: 团队的权重表与单独的权重表不同：重量级组件（首先是编辑器）是用 React 编写的，因此更改运行时意味着重写加上完整的回归传递，而 QA 时间的成本比任何框架差异都多。

sentences.en: That alone settled the runtime.
sentences.zh: 仅此一点就解决了运行时间。

sentences.en: At the meta-framework layer they picked TanStack Start over Next.js for equally practical reasons: the product barely touches RSC, so Next.js's architectural complexity buys nothing there, and the Vite toolchain plus fully typed routing came along for free.
sentences.zh: 在元框架层，他们出于同样实际的原因选择了 TanStack Start 而不是 Next.js：该产品几乎不涉及 RSC，因此 Next.js 的架构复杂性在那里没有任何意义，而且 Vite 工具链和完全类型化路由是免费提供的。

sentences.en: Loren's table prices that choice at 58 kB: React 19 either way, 118.2 against 176.1, a third of the bytes.
sentences.zh: Loren 的表格将这一选择定价为 58 kB：无论哪种方式，React 19 都是 118.2 对 176.1，即三分之一的字节。

blocks.html_en: • Pick frameworks by client-side bytes. AI erased learning curves from the weight table. Runtime cost lives in the architecture, and no prompt removes it.

content_html_zh:
发布 KeyEcho 1.0 的 130 个文件 PR 包含一个我从未写过的决定：桌面应用程序从 Tauri 1 + Vue 迁移到 Tauri 2 + SolidJS。

同年夏天，upweb.dev 从 Nuxt 4 迁移到 SolidStart，keyecho.app（SSR、五种语言、Stripe checkout）从头开始在 SolidStart 上构建。 三个独立的决定，每次都有相同的答案。

首先是范围。 这篇文章是关于我个人项目的默认设置。 接近尾声时，我将介绍我仍然使用 React 的两个地方，其中之一运行得很好。

标准改变了

我的仓库中的大部分代码现在都是人工智能生成的。 我的工作已经从编写代码转变为审查代码。

旧的框架清单主要关注学习曲线和开发人员经验。 现在两者的价值都为零：该模型流利地编写了五个框架，并且比大多数人更了解钩子规则。 人工智能降低了编写代码的成本。 有两项成本没有变化：每个用户下载的运行时字节数，以及审查所花费的人力时间。 根据新价格选择您的堆栈。

运行时会消耗架构中的生命。 没有提示可以删除它，因此它决定了框架。 审查带宽是有限的，人工智能吞吐量会降低通过口头协议维持的任何一致性，因此它决定了样式层。 我在几年后见过不止一个 React 代码库：两个动画库、两个轮播组件、三个样式系统，每一个都在某些票据上出于充分的理由而引入，总之就是一团乱，没人能收拾干净。 人工智能并没有发明这种漂移。 它使其速度快了一个数量级。

React 不再是我的默认设置：大小和性能

让我先放弃那些薄弱的论点。 依赖数组、过时的闭包、重新渲染风暴：我不会对其中任何一个重新提出异议。 该模型知道 hooks 的规则，eslint-plugin-react-hooks 可以捕获大多数错误，并且 React Compiler 现在可以处理记忆化。 人体工程学争论不再产生胜利者。

仍然能产生赢家的是客户。 react-dom 压缩后大约为 40 kB，是一个虚拟 DOM 加上一个协调器，在每个用户的每次冷访问时下载、解析和执行。 React Compiler 可以消除浪费的重新渲染。 它无法删除运行时本身。 开发者从来感受不到这个成本。 手机用户不断付费。

我的项目是一个人加上一群代理。 招聘池对我没有帮助，内容网站几乎没有触及组件库生态系统。 一旦权重发生这样的变化，React 在我的个人堆栈中就没有剩余位置了。

同一个应用程序，写了十次

Loren Stewart 去年进行了一项实验：在十个框架中实现相同的看板应用程序，具有相同的功能（拖放、评论、标签、服务器验证），然后比较看板页面上的压缩 JS。

框架 压缩JS
Next.js 16（反应 19） 176.1 kB
TanStack 启动 (React 19) 118.2 kB
模拟（角度 20） 103.9 kB
Nuxt 4（视图 3） 74.7 kB
TanStack Start + Solid 60.4 kB
奎克城 58.4 kB
SvelteKit (Svelte 5) 54.1 kB
SolidStart（SolidJS 1.9） 41.5 kB
天文 + HTMX 34.3 kB
Marko 28.8 kB

有两个比较很突出。 相同的元框架 TanStack Start，将 React 替换为 Solid：118.2 kB 变为 60.4 kB。 Next.js 与 SolidStart：4.2 倍的差距。 他的结论是，React 的架构成本是任何元框架都无法优化掉的。

边界，一如既往。 所有十个实现的 FCP 都在 35 到 71 毫秒之间，凭感觉无法区分。 实际站点的瓶颈通常是图像和视频，框架 JS 只占总字节数的一小部分。 但这两种成本的表现不同：媒体优化是在网站生命周期内持续进行的工作，而框架权重则永远固定在 pnpm create 上。 人工智能已经降低了编写代码的成本。 对于相同的功能集，没有理由一开始就重四倍。

第一方号码、同一站点、之前和之后。 在 Nuxt 4 上，upweb.dev 在移动 PageSpeed 上的得分为 70/100/100/100。 我在Cloudflare层做了一轮又一轮的调优，性能分数没有变化；实验室的瓶颈一直指向 JS 的重量。 转移到 SolidStart 后：99/100/100/100，模拟 Moto G Power 慢速 4G、FCP 1.5 秒、LCP 1.8 秒、CLS 0.006、TBT 0 毫秒。 零 TBT 是细粒度反应性最能带来回报的地方：主线程上没有需要协调的虚拟树。

在引用之前先从这个比较中挤出水分：灯塔实验室数据、单页会话、一个站点的样本，并且框架并不是唯一的变量。 人们会提醒你，实验室分数并不是真正的用户体验，他们是对的。 RUM 告诉您用户今天的表现如何；实验室会告诉您该架构还剩下多少空间。 相同的域名，相同的 CDN 配置，交换了框架，分数从 70 变成了 99。 免费积分。 我拿走了它们。

桌面遵循相同的逻辑：在 Tauri WebView 内，运行时减少的每一千字节都会作为冷启动时间和内存返回。

我确实看过 Svelte、Vue、Qwik 和 Marko

Svelte 5 的符文也是信号，SvelteKit 的 54.1 kB 已经足够小了。 Vue 的反应性来自同一个家族，Nuxt 的 74.7 kB 是三大元框架中最好的结果。 排除它们的是语言层。

.svelte 和 .vue 是自定义文件格式。 TypeScript 是文件内的一个区域，类型检查通过一座桥运行：一侧是 svelte-language-tools，另一侧是 Volar。 这座桥每天都运转良好。 当工具链移动时，账单就会到达。 TypeScript 7.0 于本月 8 日发布 GA，这是一个原生 Go 编译器，官方公布速度提高了 10 倍左右，VS Code 代码库的类型检查从 125.7 秒降至 10.6 秒。 JSX 是普通的 TS，因此 Solid 项目会在发布之日进行切换，零更改。 Vue和Svelte模板检查所依赖的编译器API在7.0中没有稳定版本。 他们等待 10 月的 7.1。 每个额外的语言层都是升级队列中的另一个位置。

还有一个针对 Nuxt 的问题：流式 SSR 仍然被锁定在实验性 ssrStreaming 标志后面，而 SolidStart 中的流式传输是 createHandler 的三种内置渲染模式之一。 没有旗帜可以翻转。

Qwik 的可恢复性解决了巨大的、交互稀疏的登陆页面的问题，而我的网站则没有，而且我不想采用它的思维模型。 Marko 的 28.8 kB 是十个网站中最小的，但对于我计划维护多年的两个网站来说，这个生态系统太小了。

反应模型已经趋同。 Vue refs、Svelte 5 runes、Angular 和 Preact 信号，加上 TC39 Signals 提案，都指向相同的方向。 React 是唯一的例外，它通过编译器进行修补。 Signals、JSX、plain TS：同时要求这三个，剩下的就是 Solid。

Astro 比 SolidStart 小。 为什么不是阿斯特罗？

该表中的一行值得直接回答：Astro + HTMX 为 34.3 kB，比 SolidStart 小 7 kB。 我的两个网站都是内容丰富的营销网站。 仅凭字节逻辑，Astro 就获胜了。

Astro的模型是内容优先，默认零JS，通过岛屿添加交互性。 问题是，当您编写交互部分时，每个岛都会托管一个组件框架，并且该项目现在讲两种方言：用于页面结构的 .astro 模板、用于行为的岛框架、跨接缝传递的状态。 .astro 是另一种自定义文件格式，因此上一节中的升级队列问题也适用于此。

这两个网站实际上都不是静态的。 keyecho.app 运行 Stripe 结帐、订阅流程和五种语言的 SSR，并且即将推出声音包销售页面。 upweb.dev 已悄然成长为一个小型系统：双重选择加入订阅、帖子和时事通讯档案均在 Broadside 中管理，每次发布时都会有一个 Webhook 访问该网站，验证其签名、重复数据删除、清除服务器内容缓存，然后通过缓存标签清除 Cloudflare。 新文章无需部署即可上线。 所有这些都存在于 SolidStart API 路由中，交互密度从此只会上升。 一个涵盖静态和动态部分的组件模型对我来说价值 7 kB。

如果您的网站是纯粹的博客或文档，并且每年更改 3 次，那么 Astro 是更好的答案，而不是 SolidStart。 对于更大的团队，最佳实践是彻底拆分：Astro 或 SolidStart 上的登录页面，以及具有自己堆栈的其自己的子域上的控制台。

生态系统法案：TanStack 涵盖了其中最糟糕的部分，即您自己开发的组件

SolidStart 并不是灵丹妙药，它最大的弱点是生态系统。 React 拥有的大多数东西，Solid 都有一个等价的东西，成熟度降低了一层，选择也更少。 你可以忍受其中的一部分。 其中一部分有解决方法。

覆盖范围最广的解决方法是 TanStack。 Router、Query、Form、Table、Virtual：每个包都是一个核心加上适配器，Solid 是一流的。 我在 difflore.dev 的生产环境中运行 Solid 版本的 TanStack Start：solid-router、solid-query 和 oRPC，一个带有身份验证、数据库和 AI 调用的完整后端，到目前为止没有丢失任何部件。 该供应商划掉了清单上最重的物品。

更大的趋势也有所帮助：基础设施正在变得与框架无关。 Vite、Nitro、Drizzle、Zod、oRPC、better-auth 并不关心你的 UI 的渲染内容，无头 UI 已经从 React-only 转向带有适合所有人的适配器的状态机核心。 生态系统中框架独有的份额正在缩小。 另一方面值得大声说出来：你从仅限 React 的库中获得的每一项便利都会增加你必须爬过才能离开的墙。 生态系统既是资产，又是枷锁。

剩下的差距是完善的组件库。 React 方面的标准是 shadcn/ui； Solid 有社区端口，但牵引力只是其中的一小部分。 这并没有让我放慢速度，shadcn 本身就说明了原因：它不附带 npm 包。 它将组件源复制到您的存储库中，将组件从依赖项转换为您自己的代码。 像这样的定义正是人工智能快速产生的。 一个组件写了两次，一个 React 版本和一个 Solid 版本，需要几分钟的时间。 我当前的界面都没有使用预构建的库：两个站点都是在 token 之上手写的组件，React 管理员使用 Base UI，Solid 端使用 Kobalte，所有无头原语的外观和行为都由我定义。 AI解决了输出问题。 它没有解决的是如何设计组件 API、状态应该存在于何处以及如何强制执行令牌。 该技能可以在 React 和 Solid 之间完全转移，并且组件库护城河越浅，就越值得练习。

样式：实用程序类失去了默认插槽

首先是当前状态，所以这读起来不像是删除：KeyEcho 桌面版在 UnoCSS 上发布了 1.0，今天仍在运行，而且我没有计划将其删除。 该 UI 很小且固定，没有可匹配的设计文件，并且面板一旦确定就很少更改，因此实用程序类在那里几乎没有出错的空间。 管理平台和营销网站的形状不同：页面不断增加，样式跟踪重新设计，并且有一种设计语言需要捍卫。 下面的两种失效模式属于第二种形状。

实用程序类在人工智能手中失败有两点。

首先是token漂移，Tailwind和UnoCSS同样暴露。 该项目有自己的设计标记，模型仍然达到其训练内存中的默认比例，或者下降到任意值。 在审查中，gap-3 和gap-[12px] 看起来几乎相同，今天它们的渲染效果完全相同。 不同的是，第二个已经离开了代币系统，因此下一次规模变更会错过它。 每条线都是合法的，并且设计系统为每个 PR 分叉更多一点。 我不止一次看到这种情况发生。 Tailwind v4 的 @theme 清理了 token 定义；任意值永远合法。

第二是尺寸。 原子 CSS 依赖于类重用：样式表应该随着页数的增加而停止增长。 任意值太多了，这个前提就崩溃了。 每个 w-[347px] 都是一次性规则，样式表随着 PR 计数而增长，HTML 中的类字符串也是如此。 这种宽松的约束模型可以编译所有内容，而成本则在其他地方计费。

vanilla-extract 将 token 转换为 TypeScript 导出。 样式位于 .css.ts 文件中，颜色必须从主题导入，拼写错误的变量是类型错误，从内存中发明的十六进制值无法通过类型检查。 upweb.dev 上的主题合约通过 createThemeContract 定义了 16 个代币；浅色和深色必须分别分配每个键，并且缺少一个深色值会导致构建失败。 该规则不再取决于任何人的记忆，或模型是否阅读文档。 输出是纯 CSS，运行时间为零，按路线分割：访问者仅下载当前页面使用的样式，而不是站点范围的工作表。

关于 CSS-in-TS 最古老的抱怨是命名。 人工智能翻转了这一点：模型进行命名，我只进行审查，并且命名块的读取速度比三十个字符的实用字符串更快。

CSS 字段的其余部分

零运行时间是下限，它立即清除运行时间逼近。 styled-components 和 Emotion 在浏览器中生成样式，需要额外的 SSR 序列化，并且 styled-components 正式处于维护模式。 CSS 模块是零运行时的，但标记是类型系统看不到的字符串约定，因此主题契约是通过希望来强制执行的。

Panda CSS 和 StyleX 属于 vanilla-extract 的范畴：类型化、编译时。

熊猫失去了运营形态。 它代码生成了整个样式系统输出目录，每当标记发生更改时都必须重新生成该目录，并且调用站点看起来像一个运行时库，同时表现得像构建时静态提取，因此任何动态对象形状（计算的键，从函数传播）都会从样式表中悄悄删除。 内容网站的管道太多。

StyleX 的设计没有任何问题，defineVars 强制执行代币也很好，但它陷入了生态系统状态：两个社区 Vite 插件相继死亡，官方的 unplugin 集成迟到，SolidStart 中没有既定路径。 对于基础设施，我只选择当前构建链视为一等公民的基础设施。 vanilla-extract 的官方 vite 插件多年来一直保持稳定，并且可以嵌入到任何基于 Vite 的框架中。

React 仍然运行的地方

我日常工作中开发的一个高度交互的产品在 TanStack Start 上运行 React 19 和 React Compiler，并且运行良好。 团队的权重表与单独的权重表不同：重量级组件（首先是编辑器）是用 React 编写的，因此更改运行时意味着重写加上完整的回归传递，而 QA 时间的成本比任何框架差异都多。 仅此一点就解决了运行时间。 在元框架层，他们出于同样实际的原因选择了 TanStack Start 而不是 Next.js：该产品几乎不涉及 RSC，因此 Next.js 的架构复杂性在那里没有任何意义，而且 Vite 工具链和完全类型化路由是免费提供的。 Loren 的表格将这一选择定价为 58 kB：无论哪种方式，React 19 都是 118.2 对 176.1，即三分之一的字节。

Broadside 的管理员则是另一回事。 它还使用 TanStack Start 运行 React，但有一个不太讨人喜欢的原因：我需要运行平台，但没有时间切换堆栈。 计划是将其转移到 TanStack Start 的 Solid 适配器上，与 difflore.dev 的组合相同：保留生态系统，交换运行时。

Broadside 是我自托管的时事通讯平台：内置 MCP 服务器，代理草稿，模型观看数字，我审阅并按发送。 一旦清理完毕，我就会将其开源。 那就单独发帖吧。

三项决定的五项规则

  1. 按客户端字节选择框架。 人工智能从体重表中删除了学习曲线。 运行时成本存在于体系结构中，没有任何提示可以将其删除。
  2. 仅当编译器强制执行时，约束才有效。 在 AI 吞吐量中不存在默默失败的规则。 文档在长时间的会话中会被稀释；类型错误则不会。
  3. 每个额外的语言层都是升级队列中的另一个位置。 TS 7 本月：JSX 第一天启用，模板 DSL 等到 10 月份。
  4. 更喜欢与框架无关的生态系统，并发展自己的组件层。 单一框架库的便利性和它的锁定来自同一个地方。 无头原语加上你自己的代币：人工智能提供数量，你提供规则。
  5. 改变默认，不要对过去耿耿于怀。 UnoCSS 仍然运行桌面应用程序，日常工作的 React 产品运行良好。 新的默认值适用于新存储库的第一行。

upweb.dev 和 keyecho.app 目前都在 SolidStart + vanilla-extract 上运行。 我打开的下一个存储库可能也会从那里开始，直到某个项目的形状再次改变答案。
